hero.subtitle: GreenMind analyzes plant bioelectrical signals to detect stress earlier and specifically improve cultivation strategies.

problem.title2: when the damage is already visible.
problem.description: Today, sensors monitor the environment — temperature, humidity, soil moisture. But not the plant itself. However, plants send out bioelectrical signals that indicate stress — often before external symptoms become visible.

solution.title: Understanding plant signals. Acting more specifically.
solution.description: GreenMind records bioelectrical signals directly on the plant and makes them usable for data-driven cultivation decisions.

earlyStress.title: Early stress detection
earlyStress.description: Bioelectrical signals can indicate drought, pest, and nutrient stress — aiming to identify problems before visible damage occurs.

dataPlanning.title: Data-driven yield planning
dataPlanning.description: Plant signals combined with environmental data enable new approaches for yield planning — data-driven instead of purely experience-based.

resources.title: Targeted resource usage
resources.description: When the plant signals what it needs, water and nutrients can be used as needed — lower consumption, better results.

howItWorks.title: From signal to recommendation.
howItWorks.description: Plants react to their environment with measurable electrical signals. GreenMind makes these signals visible and analyzable.

s1.title: Plant signal
s1.description: Plants generate electrical signals in response to changes in their environment — light, water, temperature, nutrients.

s2.title: Sensors
s2.description: Sensors on the plant capture these signals non-invasively and combine them with environmental data like temperature and soil moisture.

s3.title: Analysis
s3.description: The data is automatically processed, filtered, and examined for patterns — which signals indicate which stress?

s4.title: Recommendation
s4.description: Insights are translated into concrete action recommendations — when to water, when to fertilize, when to intervene.

foundation.title: Scientifically founded. Field-tested.
foundation.description: Developed in collaboration with FHNW and building on peer-reviewed research in plant electrophysiology.
foundation.f1: University of Applied Sciences Northwestern Switzerland

i1.title: Early stress detection
i1.description: Detect plant stress before it becomes visible — through the analysis of bioelectrical signal patterns.

i2.title: Better yield planning
i2.description: Plant signals and environmental data combined allow for more informed forecasts for harvest preparation.

i3.title: Targeted watering and fertilizing
i3.description: Apply water and nutrients exactly when the plant needs them — not according to a rigid schedule.

i4.title: Faster risk detection
i4.description: Identify pest infestations or diseases early and take targeted countermeasures.

i5.title: Lower resource consumption
i5.description: Reduce the ecological footprint through the demand-driven use of water and energy.

i6.title: Data-driven decisions
i6.description: From gut feeling to evidence — make cultivation decisions based on real plant data.

cta.description: Do you operate a cultivation environment and want to become part of the project? We are looking for partners for field tests and joint development.
